pick.rationale: Illinois enters as a legitimate top-7 team nationally with the #1 adjusted offensive efficiency in KenPom (131.2) and #2 in Torvik, while North Carolina sits 27th-29th in the major metrics—a gap of roughly 22 spots that translates to over 11 points per 100 possessions. The Tar Heels are significantly compromised without star freshman Caleb Wilson, a potential NBA lottery pick who underwent season-ending thumb surgery, and they went just 5-3 without him including an ACC Tournament quarterfinal loss. Illinois dominated Penn 88-64 in the first round behind freshman Keaton Wagler's 26 points, showcasing their offensive firepower against a UNC defense that ranks just 34th nationally. The Illini's elite offense should exploit the shorthanded Tar Heels, while Illinois's 24th-ranked defense can contain a UNC attack that lacks its primary playmaker.
